acr.summary: Manage private registries with Azure Container Registries.

aro.summary: Manage Azure Red Hat OpenShift clusters.

functionapp.summary: Manage function apps. To install the Azure Functions Core tools see https://github.com/Azure/azure-functions-core-tools.

compute-recommender.summary: Manage sku/zone/region recommender info for compute resources.

cache.summary: Commands to manage CLI objects cached using the `--defer` argument.

cosmosdb.summary: Manage Azure Cosmos DB database accounts.

dls.summary: Manage Data Lake Store accounts and filesystems.

dms.summary: Manage Azure Data Migration Service (classic) instances.

eventgrid.summary: Manage Azure Event Grid topics, domains, domain topics, system topics partner topics, event subscriptions, system topic event subscriptions and partner topic event subscriptions.

keyvault.summary: Manage KeyVault keys, secrets, and certificates.

managedservices.summary: Manage the registration assignments and definitions in Azure.

mysql.summary: Manage Azure Database for MySQL servers.

policy.summary: Manage resources defined and used by the Azure Policy service.

redis.summary: Manage dedicated Redis caches for your Azure applications.

relay.summary: Manage Azure Relay Service namespaces, WCF relays, hybrid connections, and rules.

mariadb.summary: Manage Azure Database for MariaDB servers.

role.summary: Manage Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC).

ad.summary: Manage Microsoft Entra ID (formerly known as Azure Active Directory, Azure AD, AAD) entities needed for Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC) through Microsoft Graph API.

security.summary: Manage your security posture with Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

group.summary: Manage resource groups and template deployments.

deployment.summary: Manage Azure Resource Manager template deployment at subscription scope.

deployment-scripts.summary: Manage deployment scripts at subscription or resource group scope.

ts.summary: Manage template specs at subscription or resource group scope.

stack.summary: A deployment stack is a native Azure resource type that enables you to perform operations on a resource collection as an atomic unit.

managedapp.summary: Manage template solutions provided and maintained by Independent Software Vendors (ISVs).

sf.summary: Manage and administer Azure Service Fabric clusters.

sql.summary: Manage Azure SQL Databases and Data Warehouses.

synapse.summary: Manage and operate Synapse Workspace, Spark Pool, SQL Pool.

connection.summary: Commands to manage Service Connector local connections which allow local environment to connect Azure Resource. If you want to manage connection for compute service, please run 'az webapp/containerapp/spring connection'.

snapshot.summary: Manage point-in-time copies of managed disks, native blobs, or other snapshots.

vm.summary: Manage Linux or Windows virtual machines.

vmss.summary: Manage groupings of virtual machines in an Azure Virtual Machine Scale Set (VMSS).

sshkey.summary: Manage ssh public key with vm.

configure.summary: Manage Azure CLI configuration. This command is interactive.

feedback.summary: Send feedback to the Azure CLI Team.

find.summary: I'm an AI robot, my advice is based on our Azure documentation as well as the usage patterns of Azure CLI and Azure ARM users. Using me improves Azure products and documentation.

interactive.summary: Start interactive mode. Installs the Interactive extension if not installed already.

logout.summary: Log out to remove access to Azure subscriptions.

version.summary: Show the versions of Azure CLI modules and extensions in JSON format by default or format configured by --output.
